crime: Whatever the girls needed done went through the go-between girl, and the go-between passed every one of those asks on to her. The go-between carried nothing herself — she held the solo cell.

On days the pile grew past starting, she folded into the doll on her bunk and let the hours pass. The girls knew the cloth doll on her pillow whenever she was elsewhere; each time she came back, the pile had grown.

That evening the go-between promised the girls the week's washing by morning and tipped all of it onto her before the whole lounge. The go-between demanded her doll as forfeit — it would sit in the solo cell tonight; done by morning, or it went in the lounge fire.
In her own cell she rolled her spare clothes into a sleeping shape under her blanket. In the empty solo cell, on the shelf, she folded.
The go-between came back at lock, found the doll on the shelf, and held it up at the bars for the neighbor-cell girl — "she actually brought it." The door was locked from the outside.
In the locked hours she became a girl again, pressed the bedding down over the go-between's face, and held it. She folded back onto the shelf.

The guard's unlock round passed a sleeping shape in her cell. After the morning stream she stepped out of the solo cell; one girl saw her at its doorway. Mid-morning the go-between was found cold under her own bedding — smothered in the locked hours, inside a cell whose barred door admits no arm as far as the bunk.

Her cellmate woke in the night and touched the sleeping shape: rolled clothes. The doll — demanded before the whole lounge, shown at the bars at lock — was gone from the sealed cell; no girl had ever seen her and the doll at the same time. The vanished doll and the girl in no bed were one: she was the doll locked in with the go-between, and the hands that held the bedding down were hers.
fields.epitaph: The days always came all at once, and she could only ever be carried — the one road she went alone led into that locked cell.
execution: Over the opened pit, a long raised iron way ran to a far platform. At the near end stood a rank of iron porter-hands.
The hands took her up and bore her — smooth, unhurried — the first stretch of the way, then set her on her feet at its middle and folded back empty, pair by pair.
Behind her, the way began to fold away, plate by plate, at the machine's own pace. Ahead, the way ran on, plain and walkable, to the platform.
She turned back and raised her arms to the folding hands. No hand returned. She did not walk.
The way folded from under where she stood, and the pit took her.

magic.name: the Doll-Fold
magic.text: At her will she folds into a small cloth doll — a made thing to every eye and hand, which sees and hears as she does, goes only where hands carry it, and becomes a girl again only at her own will.
